description: You are working as a platform-engineer entrusted with a small maintenance chore for an existing CI/CD workspace that lives in the default home directory /home/user. Please bring the workspace into the **exact** state described below. The automated grader will validate every path, filename, and file-content byte for an exact match, so follow the specifications precisely. 1. Workspace layout to end up with • a directory /home/user/pipelines containing two sub-directories scripts and configs. • the directory tree must therefore look like this when you finish (use tree or find to reassure yourself, but it is not mandatory to show those commands): /home/user/pipelines ├── configs └── scripts 2. An **existing** file called /home/user/sample.env must be moved (not copied) into the new configs directory and simultaneously renamed to production.env. • Final absolute path: /home/user/pipelines/configs/production.env • The original file must no longer be present in /home/user once you are done. 3. Create a Bash script named deploy.sh inside scripts with exactly the three lines shown below (including newline placement and final newline at EOF): #!/usr/bin/env bash echo "Deploy script executed" • File must reside at /home/user/pipelines/scripts/deploy.sh. • Make the script executable. 4. Generate a plain-text audit log file at /home/user/pipelines/pipeline.log that **exactly** contains the two lines below, in the same order, each followed by a single newline (i.e. the file ends with a newline character): CONFIG: /home/user/pipelines/configs/production.env SCRIPT: /home/user/pipelines/scripts/deploy.sh No additional spaces, lines, or characters are allowed. Verification checklist used by the autograder (for your convenience): • Directory structure is correct. • production.env exists at the specified path and the original sample.env has been removed from /home/user. • deploy.sh exists, has the exact two-line body shown above, and has its executable bit set. • pipeline.log exists with the two lines shown above and nothing else. Tip: everything can be accomplished with 3-4 straightforward commands (mkdir, mv, a redirection to create the script, a chmod, and a redirection to create the log file). Feel free to use additional commands (e.g. cat, ls) for your own verification, but ensure the final state matches the checklist.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing material in the container BEFORE the agent starts
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <file path="/home/user/sample.env"><![CDATA[# Sample environment file
]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <!--
        Expected final state AFTER the agent completes the task
    -->
    <final_state>
        <!-- Directories -->
        <directory path="/home/user/pipelines"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/pipelines/scripts"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/pipelines/configs"/>

        <!-- Moved & renamed file -->
        <file path="/home/user/pipelines/configs/production.env"><![CDATA[# Sample environment file
]]></file>

        <!-- Bash script -->
        <file path="/home/user/pipelines/scripts/deploy.sh" mode="755"><![CDATA[#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo "Deploy script executed"
]]></file>

        <!-- Audit log -->
        <file path="/home/user/pipelines/pipeline.log"><![CDATA[CONFIG: /home/user/pipelines/configs/production.env
SCRIPT: /home/user/pipelines/scripts/deploy.sh
]]></file>

        <!-- File that must NOT exist any longer -->
        <absent path="/home/user/sample.env"/>
    </final_state>
